candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture’s core no-go step be made precise: is there a rigorous inconsistency theorem showing that a finite region cannot have fully quantum operational content while retaining a single definite classical metric, without adding collapse, hidden variables, or nonlinear dynamics? This is worth pursuing because the whole conjecture turns on whether options (1) and (3) are merely unattractive or actually impossible.

What exact deeper quantum-relational structure could generate classical spacetime, and by what mechanism do metric, locality, and causal order emerge from it? This is the constructive gap in the conjecture: saying spacetime is emergent is not yet an explanation unless one can specify the substrate, its dynamics, and the conditions under which GR appears as an effective theory.

Why should emergent geometry couple universally to all forms of energy/information, yielding the equivalence principle and Einstein-like dynamics rather than some non-universal effective force? This is a major unresolved tension because many emergent-spacetime ideas can reproduce some geometric features, but far fewer explain gravity’s universality as a necessary consequence rather than an added assumption.
